objective: Translate tutorial principles into executable PPT renderer actions.

execution_contract.minimum_mapping_rule: each tutorial_rule_mapping must contain at least one renderer_action with action_type, target_selector, parameters, fallback_strategy, and traceability back to tutorial, visual, and motion source ids
execution_contract.non_goal: QA-only advice such as 'make it clearer' without layout, style, structure, or timing actions

select_element.purpose: Resolve a role or semantic selector to one or more slide elements.

set_geometry.purpose: Move, resize, or anchor a visual or text element in slide percentage coordinates.

set_style.purpose: Change visual salience through opacity, stroke, fill, font size, weight, or contrast.

remove_element.purpose: Delete non-essential decorative or duplicate elements from the slide.

hide_element.purpose: Keep an element in the document but make it invisible in the current build or slide.

reorder_element.purpose: Adjust z-order so primary objects read before supporting material.

crop_media.purpose: Crop an image or video to emphasize the meaningful object or detail.

align_elements.purpose: Snap related objects to a shared edge, centerline, or baseline.

distribute_spacing.purpose: Normalize gaps between repeated elements or between content blocks.

group_elements.purpose: Combine a visual and its dependent labels into a stable layout unit.

duplicate_slide.purpose: Create a build slide from a previous slide so a single change can be staged cleanly.

create_comparison_layout.purpose: Create before/after, old/new, or problem/solution contrast on one slide or slide pair.

add_reveal_step.purpose: Convert dense simultaneous content into ordered click/build reveals.

add_animation.purpose: Attach a specific animation effect to an element or group.

set_timing.purpose: Set or adjust relative timing for existing build steps.

set_transition.purpose: Set slide transition style and duration for rhythm between slides.

composite_preview.purpose: Render the slide/build preview after structural, visual, or timing changes.

visual_hierarchy.definition: Make the primary idea visually dominant before supporting detail.
ppt_operations: place the main object on a strong anchor
raise the main object in z-order
delay labels until after the object is seen

white_space.definition: Create quiet space around the message so the slide breathes and the eye has a path.
ppt_operations: delete secondary boxes and decorative containers
reduce strokes, fills, and background noise

keynote_rhythm.definition: Turn a dense explanation into a staged slide sequence with clear beats.
ppt_operations: one idea per click or duplicate slide
build setup, change, result as separate beats
hold the visual before explaining it
reveal labels after the viewer has seen the object
use slide duplication for state changes instead of stacking everything

motion_pacing.definition: Make motion support comprehension by controlling order, speed, delay, and pauses.
ppt_operations: animate object movement before label reveal
use short fades or moves for minor changes
use longer pauses after major reveals
avoid simultaneous motion on competing elements
match motion direction to reading or causal flow

tutorial_rule_mappings.tutorial_rule: The main object must win the first read.
tutorial_rule_mappings.design_move: Scale and anchor the primary subject, then reduce nearby competitors.
ppt_operations: move main object to visual anchor

renderer_actions.fallback_strategy: choose the largest non-background image or grouped shape
scale around current center by 1.15 if fixed coordinates would crop the element
skip style change when element contains required body text

anti_patterns: add a note saying the hierarchy should be clearer without changing size, position, or contrast

tutorial_rules.derived_rule: Each slide must declare the one decision-driving object before any proof detail is drawn.
tutorial_rules.observed_design_move: The decisive claim is isolated first, then secondary evidence is delayed or routed away from the main canvas.

tutorial_rules.derived_rule: The climax slide must reserve the largest canvas zone for one native hero object before labels and secondary proof appear.
tutorial_rules.observed_design_move: The strongest proof object expands while explanation waits until the focal object is understood.

visual_targets.desired_behavior: The full-skill arm shows a native before/after visual delta: the before side is visibly flat, the after side enlarges one supported proof object, removes nonessential labels, and routes detail to notes or trace.
The full-skill arm gives the climax the largest supported outcome object and makes the close a decision handoff with a visible public-blocked release gate.

motion_targets.desired_behavior: The climax should scale one native outcome object above prior proof objects and let minor labels become subordinate.

tutorial_rule_mappings.tutorial_rule: Frames that do not carry meaning should not compete with the subject.
tutorial_rule_mappings.design_move: Delete or fade secondary boxes, borders, and card shells around non-primary content.
ppt_operations: keep only the frame that defines the main comparison or crop

renderer_actions.fallback_strategy: set stroke_width_px to 0 if the renderer cannot safely delete the object
apply only opacity reduction if stroke controls are unavailable

anti_patterns: wrap every point in a box because the tutorial mentioned grouping

tutorial_rules.derived_rule: Each slide must declare the one decision-driving object before any proof detail is drawn.
tutorial_rules.observed_design_move: The decisive claim is isolated first, then secondary evidence is delayed or routed away from the main canvas.

tutorial_rules.derived_rule: Every proof object must use original naming, neutral shapes, and traceable source-boundary checks.
tutorial_rules.observed_design_move: Commercial evidence supports the workflow claim while brand-specific surface details are removed.

visual_targets.desired_behavior: The full-skill arm shows a native before/after visual delta: the before side is visibly flat, the after side enlarges one supported proof object, removes nonessential labels, and routes detail to notes or trace.

motion_targets.desired_behavior: The before thumbnail should reveal first, the after thumbnail should reveal second with a larger proof object, and the delta label should explain the edit.

tutorial_rule_mappings.tutorial_rule: Layer order should make the reading order obvious.
tutorial_rule_mappings.design_move: Bring primary subject and active annotation forward; push context, shadows, and background back.
ppt_operations: place annotation above subject but below headline

renderer_actions.fallback_strategy: increase z_index to highest visual z_index plus 1
set opacity to 0.4 when z-order is locked

anti_patterns: use animation order to fix a static z-order problem while the slide still opens cluttered

tutorial_rules.derived_rule: Before generation, assign each text object to a type role with a minimum size and a maximum line length.
tutorial_rules.observed_design_move: Headline, proof phrase, label, and note are separated by scale and placement instead of color alone.

tutorial_rules.derived_rule: Each slide role must receive selected decomposition units, executable memory bindings, and gate checks before native PPT code runs.
tutorial_rules.observed_design_move: The design decision is named before implementation, so code renders a committed layout instead of improvising.

visual_targets.desired_behavior: The full-skill arm includes original slide mini-previews built from native objects, showing selected memory ids, density budget, and release gate only where those controls clarify product behavior.

motion_targets.desired_behavior: The proof should build from headline to object to route marker, with a small pause between claim and evidence.

tutorial_rule_mappings.tutorial_rule: If the audience needs to inspect a detail, crop to that detail instead of showing the full object small.
tutorial_rule_mappings.design_move: Crop or zoom the media to the meaningful detail and move the uncropped reference to a smaller context slot.
ppt_operations: move full reference into small context position

renderer_actions.fallback_strategy: use the central 70 percent crop if no detail bbox is available
fit within safe area while preserving aspect ratio

anti_patterns: add a magnifier icon without increasing the inspectable detail size

tutorial_rules.derived_rule: Convert any long block into two to four native step objects with one visible delta per step.
tutorial_rules.observed_design_move: A dense paragraph is split into a before state, an action, and an after state with fewer labels.

tutorial_rules.derived_rule: Every proof object must use original naming, neutral shapes, and traceable source-boundary checks.
tutorial_rules.observed_design_move: Commercial evidence supports the workflow claim while brand-specific surface details are removed.

motion_targets.desired_behavior: The before thumbnail should reveal first, the after thumbnail should reveal second with a larger proof object, and the delta label should explain the edit.

tutorial_rule_mappings.tutorial_rule: Labels should explain the visual after the audience sees it, not compete with it at open.
tutorial_rule_mappings.design_move: Hide labels on the first build, then reveal them after primary visual focus is established.
ppt_operations: fade in callouts after object reveal

renderer_actions.fallback_strategy: move labels to the next duplicated slide if build-level visibility is unsupported
duplicate slide and make labels visible only on the duplicate

anti_patterns: show all labels at once and rely on the presenter to tell the audience where to look

The full-skill arm compresses long spoken or written explanation into one native headline, one short support line, and optional speaker-note or trace detail.

motion_targets.desired_behavior: The opening should pause on a sparse native visual field, then reveal the editable headline before any proof marker appears.

tutorial_rule_mappings.tutorial_rule: The slide needs an intentional quiet margin before adding more content.
tutorial_rule_mappings.design_move: Constrain content to a safe area and reserve a clear empty zone.

renderer_actions.fallback_strategy: scale content group to fit within 8 percent margins
apply horizontal spacing only when vertical reflow would overflow

anti_patterns: fill every remaining empty area with explanatory text or decoration

tutorial_rule_mappings.tutorial_rule: Repeated small labels create noise; consolidate them into one legend or one staged callout.
tutorial_rule_mappings.design_move: Group repeated labels, keep one legend, and reveal detail labels only when needed.

renderer_actions.fallback_strategy: select labels by shared font size and repeated prefixes
hide duplicate labels until a later build

tutorial_rule_mappings.tutorial_rule: Clean layouts use fewer axes and more consistent gaps.
tutorial_rule_mappings.design_move: Align blocks to shared edges and normalize gutters.

use current order and clamp to non-overlap gaps

tutorial_rule_mappings.tutorial_rule: Background texture and decorative fills should not consume the same contrast as content.

open_reconciliation_tasks: Replace role-based selectors with concrete slide element ids when the renderer emits a slide object manifest.
Bind action parameters to measured layout geometry once Part C slide-story contracts and Part D scene compiler structures exist.
Keep public release blocked until native PPT render checks prove these actions execute on generated slides.

artifact_scope.goal: Reconcile tutorial, visual learning, and motion learning inputs into renderer-action mappings for native PPT generation.
artifact_scope.quality_gate: Every tutorial rule mapping must resolve to executable renderer_actions and trace back to at least one tutorial unit, visual target, and motion target.
